message:
chore(beads): file 3 reality-gap beads (ft-xg8st, ft-hdvvo, ft-d3awp)

Reality-check sweep at HEAD 4f53f7a8 found 3 doc drifts in AGENTS.md/README.md
that accumulated since ft-f1ec3 closed (2026-04-26):

- ft-xg8st (P2): all 5 'Not Yet Implemented' dispatch line numbers stale
  by ~140 lines (claimed 22887/22946/22993/23071/23131; actual 23025/
  23072/23150/23210/23259). Behavior is correct, citations are not.
- ft-hdvvo (P3): 3 newly-extracted sub-crates absent from the 'sub-crates
  extracted' inventory (cass-types/caut-types/connector-types). Tree
  still claims '10 sub-crates extracted'; reality is 13.
- ft-d3awp (P3): 488→427 module count and 47/48 vendored count both
  inconsistent with HEAD; actual is 349 core modules and 42 vendored.

CLI commands documented in README all map to enum variants in main.rs.
'Not Yet Implemented' families still dispatch the not_implemented envelope.
Feature flags in README are a strict subset of crates/frankenterm/Cargo.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.2","title":"20-pane integration test: multi-agent detection, workflow trigger, policy gate, FTS search verification","description":"# Goal
Scale the E2E proof to 20 panes with mixed agent types, verifying all major subsystems integrate correctly under realistic multi-agent conditions.

# Background
The minimal E2E (ft-d0ez0.1) proves the basic loop. This test proves it works at moderate scale with mixed agent types, workflow triggers, policy enforcement, and FTS search.

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_20pane_integration.py

### Setup
- 20 panes via headless mux server: 7 simulated Codex, 7 Claude Code, 3 Gemini, 3 plain shell
- Each agent pane receives fixture output from crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/<agent>/
- Output mix: rate limits, errors, normal output, long-running ops

### Policy configuration (ft.toml for the test)
Pane 15 is policy-denied via safety config:
  [safety]
  require_prompt_active = false
  block_alt_screen = true
  [safety.rate_limits]
  send_text = { max_per_second = 0 }  # deny all sends to trigger rate limit
  # OR use a custom policy rule that denies pane_id=15 specifically

### Verification matrix (8 checks)
1. Delta extraction: ft robot get-text --all --tail 1 returns data for all 20 panes
2. Pattern detection: ft robot events --limit 50 returns rate-limit events for 17 agent panes
3. False positive check: ft robot events filtered to panes 17,18,19 returns ZERO events
4. Workflow trigger: ft workflow status shows at least 1 completed workflow execution
5. Policy gate: ft robot send 15 'test' returns ok:false with error.code=robot.policy_denied
6. FTS search: ft robot search 'rate limit' returns results spanning multiple panes
7. Robot Mode state: ft robot state returns all 20 panes with correct metadata (domain, title)
8. Audit trail: ft audit --decision deny shows the pane 15 denial record

### Timing
- Stagger pane output over 10 seconds to test temporal ordering
- Total duration target: under 60 seconds

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_20pane_<timestamp>.json
- Per-check: check_name, expected, actual, pass/fail, duration_ms, raw_response (first 500 chars)
- Summary: total_checks, passed, failed, elapsed_total_ms
- On failure: dump ft doctor --json, full event list, audit trail, failing check raw response

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 8 verification checks pass.
2. Zero false-positive detections on plain shell panes.
3. At least 1 completed workflow in execution log.
4. Audit trail includes policy denial with pane_id=15.
5. Structured JSON report produced.
6. Completes in under 60 seconds.
7. On failure, diagnostic output pinpoints exactly which check failed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:05.594187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:32:02.956948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:32:02.956583Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","integration","multi-agent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.2","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:05.594187Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.2","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:25.496062Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.3","title":"50-pane stress test: fleet memory pressure transitions, tiered scrollback eviction, backpressure under load","description":"# Goal
Prove fleet memory management and tiered scrollback work under sustained 50-pane load, matching the advertised 200+ pane scale claims.

# Background
The README claims '200+ panes under 1GB' and describes a 4-tier fleet memory controller. The fleet_memory_controller.rs and scrollback_tiers.rs are implemented, but never tested at scale. This test proves they work.

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_50pane_stress.py
### Setup
- 50 panes via headless mux server, each generating ~1KB/sec continuous output via script
- Per-pane memory budget configured to trigger tier transitions within 5 minutes
- Total sustained throughput: ~50KB/sec

### Phase 1: Warm-up (0-60s)
- All panes generating output
- Verify: ft robot state shows 50 panes, ft doctor --json shows Normal pressure tier

### Phase 2: Pressure build (60-180s)
- Continue output generation
- Monitor: FleetPressureTier transitions via ft doctor --json (polled every 10s)
- Expected: Normal -> Elevated as scrollback accumulates

### Phase 3: Tier transitions (180-300s)
- Continue output generation with increased rate on 10 panes
- Expected: Elevated -> Critical on high-output panes
- Verify: tiered scrollback eviction events occur (warm tier activation)

### Phase 4: Detection under load (at 240s)
- Inject rate-limit pattern into pane 25
- Measure: time from injection to ft robot events showing the detection
- Expected: detection latency < 5 seconds

### Phase 5: Robot Mode under load (at 270s)
- Time ft robot state, ft robot search, ft robot get-text responses
- Expected: state < 2s, search < 5s

### Metrics collection
- Peak RSS of ft process (via ps or /proc/self/status)
- Detection latency (injection time vs event timestamp)
- Robot Mode response latency
- Pressure tier transition timestamps
- Scrollback tier eviction counts

## Logging
- JSON metrics report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_50pane_stress_<timestamp>.json
- Per-phase: phase_name, start_time, end_time, metrics_snapshot
- Timeseries: pressure_tier sampled every 10s
- On failure: full metrics dump + ft doctor --json + process info

# Acceptance Criteria
1. ft process RSS stays under 1GB with 50 panes over 5 minutes.
2. Pattern detection latency < 5 seconds under 50-pane load.
3. Robot Mode state query < 2 seconds, search < 5 seconds under load.
4. At least one FleetPressureTier transition above Normal observed.
5. At least one tiered scrollback tier transition (hot->warm) observed.
6. No crashes, panics, or data corruption during 5-minute run.
7. Machine-readable metrics report produced with all measurements.
8. On failure, report identifies which metric breached which threshold.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:21.555623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:29.576005Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:06:29.575707Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fleet-memory","performance","scrollback","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.3","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:21.555623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.3","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:57:25.736976Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2919,"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"CI note: This 5-minute test should run in a nightly CI job (not per-push) or be triggered manually. The per-push CI can run ft-d0ez0.1 (30s) and ft-d0ez0.2 (60s). Add a .github/workflows/nightly-stress.yml job for this test.","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:05:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-d0ez0.4","title":"E2E proof for ft tx run: execute a real transactional multi-pane operation and verify audit trail","description":"# Goal
Prove that ft tx run executes real pane operations transactionally, including compensation on failure, with a complete audit trail.

# Background
The tx integration tests (ft-y9lnb.5) use MockWezterm. This E2E test uses real (or headless mux server) panes to prove the full CLI->executor->pane->audit pipeline works.

# Verified Contract Format (MissionTxContract JSON)
{
  "tx_version": 1,
  "intent": { "tx_id": "test-tx-1", "requested_by": "operator", "summary": "E2E test", "correlation_id": "e2e-001", "created_at_ms": <timestamp> },
  "plan": {
    "plan_id": "plan-1", "tx_id": "test-tx-1",
    "steps": [
      { "step_id": "step-1", "ordinal": 0, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo step1-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 0" },
      { "step_id": "step-2", "ordinal": 1, "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 1, "text": "echo step2-marker" }, "description": "Send marker to pane 1" }
    ],
    "preconditions": [],
    "compensations": [
      { "for_step_id": "step-1", "action": { "type": "send_text", "pane_id": 0, "text": "echo COMPENSATED" } }
    ]
  },
  "lifecycle_state": "pending",
  "outcome": "pending",
  "receipts": []
}

# Design
## Test script: tests/e2e/e2e_tx_run.py

### Scenario 1: Happy path (2-step transaction)
1. Start ft watch with headless mux server
2. Create 2 panes with shell sessions
3. Write mission.json contract (template above)
4. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
5. Verify: ft robot get-text 0 contains 'step1-marker'
6. Verify: ft robot get-text 1 contains 'step2-marker'
7. Verify: ft tx show --include-contract shows committed state

### Scenario 2: Partial failure with compensation
1. Same setup, but step 2 targets nonexistent pane 99
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission_fail.json
3. Verify: pane 0 contains 'COMPENSATED' (compensation ran)
4. Verify: ft tx show shows compensation state
5. Verify: ft audit shows failure + compensation records

### Scenario 3: Policy denial
1. Configure ft.toml safety to deny SendText on pane 1
2. Run ft tx run --contract mission.json
3. Verify: prepare-phase denial with policy reason in output
4. Verify: pane 0 does NOT contain 'step1-marker' (no steps executed on denial)

## Logging
- JSON report: tests/e2e/output/e2e_tx_run_<timestamp>.json
- Per-scenario: scenario_name, contract_file, ft_tx_run_stdout, ft_tx_run_stderr, ft_tx_show_output, audit_records, pane_contents, pass/fail
- On failure: full diagnostic dump

# Acceptance Criteria
1. All 3 scenarios pass.
2. Happy path shows markers in panes AND committed state in ft tx show.
3. Compensation scenario shows compensation execution in audit trail AND pane output.
4. Policy denial shows zero pane modifications.
5. Completes in under 60 seconds.
6. Structured JSON report produced.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T21:45:58.657051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T22:34:12.108953Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T22:34:12.108636Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","proof","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:45:58.657051Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:46:02.740774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-d0ez0.4","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:46:02.533076Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-d3awp","title":"[docs][AGENTS.md][README.md] workspace stats stale: '488→427 core modules' and '47/48 vendored' both wrong","description":"AGENTS.md ships several workspace-shape stats that drifted since ft-f1ec3 closed (2026-04-26):

1) Module count: AGENTS.md:273 says 'frankenterm-core shrank from 488 → 427 modules.'
   Actual top-level *.rs in crates/frankenterm-core/src: 349 (find -maxdepth 1 -name '*.rs' | wc -l).
   Delta: another ~78 modules left core in the last day, mostly via the 3 new sub-crate extractions (cass-types/caut-types/connector-types) plus their carved modules.

2) Vendored crate count: AGENTS.md gives two inconsistent claims in the SAME doc:
   - line 230: '48 vendored frankenterm/<crate>/ workspace members'
   - line 294: 'ex-WezTerm, 47 vendored'
   Reality at HEAD 4f53f7a8: find frankenterm -maxdepth 2 -name Cargo.toml | wc -l → 42 top-level
   (excludes nested codegen pieces like char-props/codegen, bidi/generate, dynamic/derive).
   Picking ONE accurate number and using it consistently is the fix — the 47/48 split inside one doc is the giveaway that the figure rotted.

3) Workspace-wide crate count is approximately right (claimed 64 in ft-f1ec3 closure; actual 19 crates/ + 42 frankenterm/ + a few derive/codegen = ~66-69) but the 488→427 module figure makes the docs feel authoritative when they are not.

Suggested fix:
   - Replace 488→427 with the verified current count (or, more durable, just say 'frankenterm-core has continued to shed modules to ft-y0loj.* sub-crates; see Cargo.toml workspace members for the live count').
   - Pick one vendored number, fix both call-sites, and add a comment '# verify with: find frankenterm -maxdepth 2 -name Cargo.toml | wc -l' so the next sweep can spot drift in 5s.

Reproducer (HEAD 4f53f7a8):
  ls -d crates/frankenterm-core-* | wc -l                           # 13 (claim: 10)
  find crates/frankenterm-core/src -maxdepth 1 -name '*.rs' | wc -l # 349 (claim: 427)
  find frankenterm -maxdepth 2 -name Cargo.toml | wc -l             # 42 (claim: 47/48)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:51.947360Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:56.448040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-d3awp","depends_on_id":"ft-f1ec3","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:56.447600Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-d6oxc","title":"add golden session restore JSON contract coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:43:56.170915Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:27.689268Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:27.688265Z","close_reason":"Verified in d6237b5d; HEAD now contains session_restore_json_contract_golden","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-d70a9","title":"claude_code.auth.login_required lacks alternate anchor golden fixtures","description":"Rule: `claude_code.auth.login_required` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:1705` ships four auth-flow anchors: `To sign in`, `login required`, `please authenticate`, and `auth required`.

Current corpus coverage under `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/claude_code/` only has one positive golden pair for the first phrasing:
- `login_required.txt`
- `login_required.expect.json`

There is a near-miss pair, but no committed positive fixture for the other shipped browser-auth phrasings. Add at least one alternate positive `.txt` + `.expect.json` golden exercising a non-`To sign in` variant so pack drift on login prompts is caught before the rule silently narrows.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:52:16.439731Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:22:20.553440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:22:20.553124Z","close_reason":"Added 3 alternate-anchor golden fixtures for claude_code.auth.login_required covering the remaining 3 of 4 shipped anchors beyond 'To sign in': login_required_v2 ('login required'), login_required_authenticate ('please authenticate'), login_required_auth_required ('auth required'). Each .txt contains a literal anchor from patterns.rs:1709-1714; each .expect.json mirrors the existing login_required.expect.json schema exactly with matched_text swapped for the covered anchor. Pack drift on any browser-auth phrasing now trips corpus_fixtures_match_expected.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dfd16","title":"[ft-y0loj.5.connector] resolve in-core connector_* importers before connector tier-2 extraction","description":"Per docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md, connector_* tier-2 extraction is blocked by FOUR in-core files that substantively consume connector types — separate from the cycle issue that the leaf-types extractions (ft-8cg6y/ft-2z15d) solve.\n\nThe importers (count from proposal Appendix):\n  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs            — 8 connector_* type refs\n  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/config.rs            — 10 connector_* type refs\n  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/chaos_scale_harness.rs — 2 connector_* refs\n  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_telemetry.rs — 6 connector_* refs\n\nproposal example (policy.rs):\n  pub credential_broker: crate::connector_credential_broker::CredentialBrokerTelemetrySnapshot\n  pub connector_governor: crate::connector_governor::GovernorSnapshot\n  pub connector_registry: crate::connector_registry::RegistryTelemetrySnapshot\n\nThis is substantive consumption (struct fields), not a façade. After the cluster moves out, these in-core files would either need to import from the new crate (creating the cargo cycle) or have their connector-specific fields broken out.\n\nRecommended fix pattern:\n  1. Audit the connector telemetry snapshot types each in-core file consumes.\n  2. Decide per-type: (a) is it a pure data type that should ride a leaf-types crate (frankenterm-core-connector-types) below core, or (b) does it logically belong to the cluster and the core consumer should be rewritten to NOT hold a connector-typed field directly?\n  3. For (a) types: extract into frankenterm-core-connector-types alongside the cass-types/caut-types leaf pattern. Both core and the new connector crate depend on the leaf — no cycle.\n  4. For (b) types: rewrite the in-core consumer to hold a generic snapshot or trait-object instead of concrete connector types.\n\nMost likely split (from quick audit): the *TelemetrySnapshot / *Status / *Health structs are leaf-friendly (pure data, Serialize/Deserialize). The *Controller / *Registry / *Mesh impls stay with the cluster.\n\nSuccess criteria:\n  1. policy.rs / config.rs / chaos_scale_harness.rs / runtime_telemetry.rs no longer reference crate::connector_*::* directly — they reach into a leaf-types crate or a generic trait.\n  2. Workspace still compiles via rch.\n  3. Unblocks the connector tier-2 cluster extraction (separate bead, post-leaf-extractions).\n\nDeps: blocked by ft-8cg6y + ft-2z15d landing first (so the leaf-types pattern is proven). Companion to ft-y0loj.5 (parked mcp/connector epic).\n\nReference: docs/proposals/ft-t2d70-mcp-connector-extraction-feasibility.md §'Survey of the connector_* cluster' + §'Why the cycle is fundamental'.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex-cobalt","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:28:05.315428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T18:50:53.656032Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T18:50:53.655836Z","close_reason":"Already shipped in f06b0e9c and a63d5bef: connector telemetry leaf types extracted and unused import fixed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","connectors","frankenterm-core","modularity","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-dfd16","depends_on_id":"ft-2z15d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:43:24.730085Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-dfd16","depends_on_id":"ft-8cg6y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:43:26.668336Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -271,6 +272,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-h90rh","title":"[security][follow-up ft-6mmyp] Persist policy-denied MCP mutations to audit_actions table (not just tracing log)","description":"## Context

ft-6mmyp primary fix shipped: mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation now emits structured tracing::warn! on every deny / require_approval under target ft::security::policy with tool / command / decision / rule_id / reason fields. That closes the 'zero observability' gap for denied MCP mutations.

The bead's ideal (fuller) fix was a record_audit_action_redacted write so denied attempts appear in the same audit_actions table as successful ones — enables SQL-queryable forensics and parity with the wa.events_annotate post-mutation audit at mcp_tools.rs:4330-4352.

## Why deferred

record_audit_action_redacted is async. The current sync gate helper at mcp_tools.rs:177-231 can't await. The callers (tx_run/rollback, mission_pause/resume/abort) all do already live inside a runtime.block_on scope, but the helper is invoked BEFORE that block_on — specifically to fail fast without opening a StorageHandle. Opening storage inside the helper on the deny path would:
  - Open a new StorageHandle (~100ms cold path)
  - Require passing db_path (currently the helper only takes &Config)
  - Add a runtime.block_on inside a sync fn that may already be inside a tokio runtime → panic risk

## Fix path

Either:
(a) Push the audit write into the deny branch of each of the 5 caller handlers (they already have runtime.block_on scopes) — duplicates ~10 lines per caller but avoids helper signature changes.
(b) Refactor mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation to return a deny-decision value and have the caller write the audit row plus the error envelope — single point of responsibility per tool.
(c) Change the helper signature to async fn mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation(config, db_path, ...) -> ... and have callers await it inside their existing runtime.block_on.

(b) is cleanest. Roughly:

    enum GateOutcome {
        Allow,
        Deny { envelope: McpResult<Vec<Content>>, audit: AuditActionRecord },
        RequireApproval { envelope: McpResult<Vec<Content>>, audit: AuditActionRecord },
    }
    fn mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation(config, summary, command_text, start) -> GateOutcome

Callers match, write the audit via their existing storage in the deny/approval branches, then return the envelope.

## Acceptance Criteria

- After deny/approval, audit_actions has a row with result='denied' or 'require_approval', decision_reason=policy_reason, action_kind matching the tool.
- Integration test per tool: seed a deny-config, call tool, assert audit row shape.
- Tracing log emission (shipped under ft-6mmyp main fix) remains in place as a second signal.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing the ft-6mmyp MVP, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:49.814251Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:54.335685Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:54.333554Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4595cc0e: storage.rs-side infrastructure for persistent policy-denial audit. Added: SCHEMA_VERSION bump 23→24, idempotent migration creating policy_denied_audit table (ts_ms/agent_id/tool_name/intent_hash/reason/reason_code/rule_id/decision + two indices), PolicyDeniedAuditRecord struct with stable reason_code + decision const, WriteCommand::RecordPolicyDenialAudit variant (+ label arm), record_policy_denial_audit_sync writer path, and StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit + cx-first sibling. Down migration is a clean DROP. Deliberately out of scope: policy.rs convenience wrapper + mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation wiring — pane 2 had a +81/-16 uncommitted diff on policy.rs (ft-eu0no shared-rate-limiter work) so I left it untouched to avoid collision. Filing ft-<new> as the wiring bead that needs both mcp_tools.rs scope and a clean-enough policy.rs; wiring test (round-trip insert+select) belongs there too.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","mcp","policy","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-h9g0q","title":"Workspace cargo check fails: web/sse.rs select! macro + frankenterm-term proptest 17-tuple","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:03:57.002490Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:04.379090Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:35:04.378252Z","close_reason":"Sweep landed across two commits: 87d4546b (error_renderer + retry non-exhaustive match after Error-variant additions) and 0b65bc0b (which absorbed my staged patches to proptest_input_serde, proptest_color_serde, proptest_keycode_serde, bintree Clone derive, and the mux 17-tuple split). Workspace check advances past the five errors the bead named. Remaining codec::proptest_codec_roundtrip 67-error block is separate scope — drifted test expectations against post-rename Pdu types, not a build-system failure.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3603,"issue_id":"ft-h9g0q","author":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","text":"Shipped mechanical fix for the error_renderer.rs:34 + retry.rs:378 non-exhaustive match sites in commit 87d4546b. Strategy: route all three new variants (RuntimeOperation, PaneOperation, WatchdogWarningRead) through the existing generic buckets (FT-9001 code, is_retryable=true) with comments at each site citing this bead.  {"id":"ft-he0w7","title":"[fuzz] add native_events::decode_wire_event fuzz target for socket wire protocol","description":"Surface: crates/frankenterm-core/src/native_events.rs:521 — fn decode_wire_event(line: &str) -> Result<Option<NativeEvent>, String>. Parses newline-delimited JSON wire events from the wezterm event socket (WireEvent::{Hello,PaneOutput,StateChange,UserVar,PaneCreated,...}). The PaneOutput variant embeds a base64-encoded data_b64 payload that becomes a Vec<u8> bounded by MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES. No existing fuzz target covers this protocol boundary.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/native_events_wire.rs
  #![no_main]
  use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
  fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
      if data.len() > 64_000 { return; }
      let Ok(line) = std::str::from_utf8(data) else { return; };
      // decode_wire_event is private. Expose via pub(crate) or a
      // test-only re-export, OR target the nearest public entry point
      // (event_stream::try_decode_line if it exists, otherwise add
      //  gated behind a 
      // feature).
      let _ = frankenterm_core::native_events::decode_wire_event_for_fuzz(line);
  });

Corpus seeds: fuzz/corpus/native_events_wire/ — one file per WireEvent variant with realistic ts/pane_id values, plus malformed-base64 PaneOutput, oversized data_b64 (>MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES to hit truncation), empty line, non-UTF-8-in-JSON-string, deeply-nested escape sequences.

Changes required: add pub(crate) fn decode_wire_event_for_fuzz in native_events.rs behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = "fuzz"))].","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:18.642190Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:19:00.447094Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:19:00.446086Z","close_reason":"Fixed in ea93a349","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-hg6io","title":"[security][follow-up ft-h90rh] Wire mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation into record_policy_denial_audit (call-site + policy.rs helper + test)","description":"## Context

ft-h90rh storage-side infra shipped in 4595cc0e: policy_denied_audit table + StorageHandle::record_policy_denial_audit async method are live. What's still missing to close the observability loop end-to-end:

1. A convenience wrapper in crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs (e.g. `fn record_policy_denial(storage: &StorageHandle, decision: &PolicyDecision, tool_name: &str, agent_id: Option<&str>, intent_hash: Option<&str>) -> Result<()>`) that builds the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord from a PolicyDecision and writes it. Skipped in the infra ship because policy.rs had an active +81/-16 uncommitted diff from pane 2's ft-eu0no rate-limiter refactor.
2. Wire the wrapper into crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs::mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation alongside the existing tracing::warn!. Challenge: the gate helper is sync; record_policy_denial_audit is async. Options:
   - (a) Gate helper returns a GateOutcome enum carrying the PolicyDeniedAuditRecord; each of the 5 callers (tx_run/rollback, mission_pause/resume/abort) — which already live inside runtime.block_on scopes — writes the row in its deny branch.
   - (b) Fire-and-forget spawn inside the sync gate via runtime_compat::spawn + block_on(None) pattern, dropping the Result.
   (a) is cleaner and per-caller controllable; (b) is smaller diff but loses errors.
3. A round-trip integration test: open a fresh DB, run migration, call record_policy_denial_audit with a known record, query it back, assert equality. Bonus: a per-tool deny-policy end-to-end test that exercises the full mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation → audit-row pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A denied wa.tx_run (or any of the 5 gated tools) under a deny policy produces both a tracing::warn! emission AND a row in policy_denied_audit queryable via SELECT.
- Integration test covering the round-trip.
- policy.rs helper has at least one unit test that builds a record from a synthetic PolicyDecision.

## Scope note

Requires mcp_tools.rs reservation and a clean-enough policy.rs to avoid collision with ft-eu0no. Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-h90rh infra, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:43:12.398456Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:00.875781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:00.875472Z","close_reason":"Partial ship in 98b8f3d4 — docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md with every authorize call site in mcp_tools.rs enumerated (12 distinct deny paths total), current deny handling per site, and a sequenced wiring plan. Actual wiring deferred: policy.rs had +81/-16 uncommitted from pane 2's ft-eu0no SharedRateLimiter refactor (PolicyEngine.rate_limiter field type change) and mcp_tools.rs had +70 uncommitted including the 6x duplicated let policy_rate_limiter cluster flagged in 810ad4c9. Shipping partial wiring under ft-hg6io would have bundled pane 2's in-flight work and likely not compiled standalone. Filed ft-rsqap as the blocked-on-ft-eu0no follow-up that picks up the matrix and actually wires the 12 deny paths once the file contention clears.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","follow-up","mcp","policy","security"]}

 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","title":"Expose live health, leak, and backpressure diagnostics through doctor, status, and robot surfaces","description":"# Goal
Make long-haul swarm health visible to operators and other agents without requiring ad hoc tracing sessions or profiler archaeology.

This task exists because instrumentation only helps users once the important signals are surfaced through the product in a stable, interpretable way. A polished finish line needs truthful, actionable health diagnostics for day-2 operations, not just internal counters buried in logs.","design":"# Design
1. Surface the signals created by instrumentation and release-gate work through operator-facing commands and machine-facing endpoints.
2. Present both concise human summaries and structured machine-readable output so humans and agents share the same truth.
3. Include the counters and state that matter for long-haul swarm operation: pane, tab, or session counts, reconnect or shutdown anomalies, backpressure or queue pressure, leak-suspicion signals, and guidance on when to escalate.
4. Back the surface with comprehensive unit coverage and at least one logged incident-style E2E scenario.
5. Keep the semantics stable enough that playbooks, docs, and acceptance scenarios can depend on them.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can inspect key long-haul health signals without attaching a profiler or reading raw logs.
2. Human and machine surfaces expose the same authoritative health state with clear field semantics.
3. Comprehensive unit tests cover aggregation, thresholding, serialization, and degraded-state handling for the new diagnostics surfaces.
4. At least one deterministic E2E or incident-rehearsal script exercises normal and degraded paths with detailed timestamped logs and stored artifacts.
5. Playbooks, docs, and operator acceptance scenarios are updated to use these surfaces as the standard diagnostic entry point.","notes":"This bead turns internal reliability work into something operators can actually use when a swarm starts to misbehave under long-haul load.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:15.364320Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:01:12.386236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:01:12.386032Z","close_reason":"Implemented HealthDiagnosticsData, HealthLeakRiskData, HealthGuidance types in robot_types.rs with from_health_snapshot() builder. Added Health variant to ApiSurface enum. 21 comprehensive unit tests covering all health levels (green/yellow/red/black), backpressure tiers, fleet pressure tiers, crash loops, leak risk fields, lock contention, guidance generation, serde roundtrip, and envelope roundtrip. All 51 robot_api_contracts + health diagnostics tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","e2e-tests","goal-line","operator","reliability","robot-mode","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.720102Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:08.319158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:15.364320Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","title":"Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints for operators","description":"# Goal
Make the project pleasant and trustworthy from the first real operator interaction: initial setup, environment validation, first successful control-plane run, and entry into recovery workflows.

This task exists because `ft-xbnl0.5.4` proves fresh-install and recovery scenarios, but the plan did not yet contain a dedicated implementation bead that improves those entrypoints themselves. A polished finish line should not merely detect that setup and recovery are rough; it should explicitly make them operator-friendly.","design":"# Design
1. Improve the first-run path so operators can get from clone or install to a validated, ready-to-use FrankenTerm workspace without reading source code or guessing about prerequisites.
2. Make environment validation and doctor output actionable, including missing dependency diagnosis, configuration bootstrap issues, restore-state problems, and recovery entrypoints.
3. Ensure the first-run and recovery surfaces point naturally into the new diagnostic, playbook, and acceptance tooling instead of acting like separate subsystems.
4. Back the flow with comprehensive unit coverage for setup or validation logic plus deterministic E2E scenarios that exercise clean-start, broken-environment, and recovery-entry cases with detailed logs and stored artifacts.
5. Keep the result honest: if some setup or recovery path is intentionally manual, document it clearly and make the product surface say so directly.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can move from clean environment to validated first successful run using a documented and product-supported path rather than tribal knowledge.
2. Missing prerequisites, bad config, restore-state issues, and other common startup failures produce actionable doctor or status output with clear next steps.
3. Recovery entrypoints route operators toward the standard diagnostic and playbook surfaces rather than leaving them in ad hoc failure states.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover setup-state detection, degraded-environment handling, and operator-facing error classification for the touched surfaces.
5. At least one deterministic E2E scenario exercises clean setup, one exercises a broken-environment or bad-config path, and one exercises recovery entry, all with detailed timestamped logging, artifact capture, and exact verification commands recorded.","notes":"This bead makes fresh-install and recovery usability an explicit implementation concern rather than something only checked at the very end by acceptance scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.049428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T17:15:33.968982Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T17:15:33.968636Z","close_reason":"operator guidance landed; artifact docs/ft-xbnl0-5-7-completion-evidence.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","e2e-tests","goal-line","operator","recovery","setup","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:12.565115Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.049428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.711759Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:12.253037Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xdt8j","title":"[bug][resource-types] backpressure_severity propagates NaN through clamp() — observe_ratio(NaN) corrupts smoothed_ratio; from_severity(NaN) leaks NaN through every output field","description":"Discovered via proptest in frankenterm-core-resource-types (AmberHare 2026-04-26). Two related defects:

1. ContinuousBackpressure::observe_ratio (backpressure_severity.rs:140) calls raw_ratio.clamp(0.0, 1.0). Rust's f64::clamp returns NaN for NaN input — it does NOT map NaN to a saturation bound. Folding NaN into the EMA poisons smoothed_ratio() permanently (NaN never decays). severity() still survives because sigmoid() has explicit NaN→0.5 handling, but operator-facing readouts (smoothed_ratio observability) become NaN.

2. ThrottleActions::from_severity (backpressure_severity.rs:88) does s = severity.clamp(0.0, 1.0); for NaN input s is NaN. Then s*s, 0.25*s, 1.0+3.0*s, 1.0-0.8*s are all NaN. Every output field violates its documented [0,1]/[1,4]/etc. range.

Reproducer: cargo test -p frankenterm-core-resource-types --test proptest_backpressure_severity -- continuous_observe_keeps_invariants throttle_actions_clamps_every_output (currently the proptest avoids NaN inputs to keep CI green; un-skip by re-adding Just(f64::NAN) to arb_ratio_sequence/arb_severity_adversarial).

Recommended fix: replace .clamp(0.0, 1.0) with an explicit NaN-aware clamp:
  let safe = if x.is_nan() { 0.0 } else { x.clamp(0.0, 1.0) };
in both observe_ratio and from_severity. Same pattern is already present in sigmoid() (returns 0.5 for NaN), so the cluster is partly hardened but not consistently.

Severity: P2 — operator dashboards can display NaN; throttling logic is unaffected because sigmoid clamps.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:10:08.091800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:20:53.074740Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:20:53.074204Z","close_reason":"Fixed in ec0160d6: explicit is_nan() guard in observe_ratio and from_severity maps NaN to least-throttled (0.0). Companion proptest re-enables NaN in adversarial strategies — 7/7 proptests + 97/97 lib tests green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-xg8st","title":"[docs][AGENTS.md] Not Yet Implemented dispatch line numbers stale by ~140 lines — all 5 ft robot families miscited","description":"AGENTS.md:498-504 'Not Yet Implemented' table cites these dispatch sites for ft robot {checkpoint, context, work, fleet, profile}:

  | ft robot checkpoint | crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:22887 |
  | ft robot context    | crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:22946 |
  | ft robot work       | crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:22993 |
  | ft robot fleet      | crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:23071 |
  | ft robot profile    | crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:23131 |

Reality at HEAD 4f53f7a8 — actual build_ntm_not_implemented_response call sites:
  line 23025 (checkpoint)
  line 23072 (context)
  line 23150 (work)
  line 23210 (fleet)
  line 23259 (profile)

All 5 numbers drifted by ~138-140 lines since the doc was written. Behavior is correct (the families do still dispatch the not_implemented envelope), but the line-number citations are wrong. AGENTS.md is the doc agents pivot on when an unfamiliar 'ft robot X' returns robot.not_implemented — wrong line numbers send them grepping the wrong region.

Repro:
  rg -nE 'build_ntm_not_implemented_response\(' crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs
  # Returns 5 lines: 23025, 23072, 23150, 23210, 23259 — none match AGENTS.md's 22887/22946/22993/23071/23131

Suggested fix: update the dispatch-site column to point at the current line numbers, OR (more durable) cite the function name 'build_ntm_not_implemented_response' and the parent enum variant ('RobotCommands::Checkpoint', etc.) so future drift is self-healing under grep.

Filed by reality-check sweep (amber-fox slug) after ft-f1ec3 closure on 2026-04-26 explicitly claimed AGENTS.md was refreshed at HEAD 5c9f62fc — so the drift accumulated in <24h.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:51.305727Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:55.806184Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap","review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xg8st","depends_on_id":"ft-f1ec3","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:34:55.805623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xgs7n","title":"[testing-fuzzing] pin extract_delta overlap-existence invariant: previous.ends_with(current[..len-delta.len()])","description":"# Gap

proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs covers 5 MRs (crash-freedom,
identity, content-suffix, pure-append, determinism) + 2 inline
regression tests, but does NOT pin the primary semantic contract of
the algorithm: **when Content(delta) is returned, the non-delta head
of `current` must be a tail of `previous`**. Formally:

    extract_delta(previous, current, _) = Content(delta)
    ⇒ previous.ends_with(&current[..current.len() - delta.len()])

This is the invariant the sliding-window search ACTUALLY proves
(search_window[pos..] == current[..overlap_len], where
search_window = previous[search_start..], so previous ends with
current[..overlap_len]). Without it, the algorithm could return a
Content(delta) where the "non-delta prefix" is pure fabrication —
the existing MR3 suffix-check proves current ends with delta but
says nothing about the overlap being real.

Adding this test catches any regression in the overlap-candidate
acceptance logic (`search_window[pos..] == current[..overlap_len]`
on ingest.rs:1711) that accidentally accepts a non-matching window.

Fix: new proptest in proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs covering
every path (pure-append fast path + sliding-window search).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:04:40.819353Z","created_by":"pane3-ingest-mr","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:32.759851Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:13:32.759521Z","close_reason":"Landed in 5eb9f956 (fix(apc, backpressure, ingest): …, overlap-existence proptest) — extract_delta_content_overlap_exists_in_previous added to tests/proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs:207. Verified: 1 passed / 0 failed / 14 filtered out on --test proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz (test run took 0.20s under 512 proptest cases). Pins the semantic contract that when Content(delta) returns, previous.ends_with(current[..current.len() - delta.len()]) — the 'overlap was real' claim that MR3's suffix-only check does not catch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ingest","proptest","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-xicum","title":"session_restore delete_session returns false after cleaning orphaned checkpoint rows","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:56.388120Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:53.130618Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:52:53.130299Z","close_reason":"delete_session now returns true when it removed orphaned checkpoint or pane-state rows, not just when the parent mux_sessions row existed; added regression coverage for orphan cleanup.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-xkpy1","title":"[ux][P3] Robot + MCP error-hint audit follow-up (beyond MCP_ERR_POLICY)","description":"## Context

/ux-audit sweep of robot + MCP surfaces. Primary finding closed as a trivial ship: all 7 MCP_ERR_POLICY deny sites in mcp_tools.rs (6 McpToolError::new sites + 1 McpEnvelope in the mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation gate helper) previously passed None as hint — leaving MCP clients with 'Read denied by policy' and nowhere to go. Now all seven route through a shared POLICY_DENY_HINT const naming two actionable recovery paths (config.safety.rules for operator review, policy_denied_audit table for decision context).

Secondary findings not in that ship, each worth a separate pass:

## 1. Robot error-code coverage drift vs README

Defined in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:4549-4564:
  robot.invalid_args, robot.unknown_subcommand, robot.config_error,
  robot.fts_query_error, robot.approval_error, robot.pane_not_found,
  robot.reservation_conflict, robot.storage_error, robot.feature_not_available,
  robot.policy_denied, robot.timeout, robot.workflow_aborted,
  robot.workflow_error, robot.workflow_not_found, robot.not_implemented,
  robot.remote_text_unavailable

README.md §Error Handling (robot mode) lists robot.wezterm_not_running and robot.require_approval — neither is a defined ROBOT_ERR_* constant. Either the README is stale (likely) or those codes exist under a different name (possible). Audit the README table against the actual constants + per-subcommand emission paths, update whichever is drifted.

## 2. Robot-mode hint uniformity

Walk every  construction in main.rs. MCP_ERR_POLICY just got treated; robot.pane_not_found, robot.timeout, robot.feature_not_available, etc. may have similar 'no-hint' patterns. Check each, add actionable hints matching the /ux-audit test: 'say what to do, not just what went wrong.'

## 3. Format-flag claims (toon vs json)

README claims  saves '40-60% fewer tokens' for AI-to-AI use. Spot-verify: render a few representative envelopes in both formats, measure actual byte counts, confirm the claim holds within some band. If TOON is actually ~15% smaller on small envelopes, the marketing text should be tightened.

## 4. Success-response conciseness

Every MCP tool envelope carries mcp_version, version, ok, data, elapsed_ms, now — 6 top-level keys. For small data payloads (e.g. a 2-item events list) the envelope can be 3-4x the size of the actual data. Consider whether any of mcp_version / version / now are redundant for the client once an initialize handshake has happened; file a separate architectural bead if so.

## Acceptance Criteria per item

- Item 1: README drift closed (either codes renamed in docs, or missing constants added in main.rs), with an assertion test that every documented code exists as a const.
- Item 2: Every robot-mode error construction reviewed; at least one shared const (like POLICY_DENY_HINT) for recurring hint families.
- Item 3: Measurement + either README update or TOON serializer improvement if claim is wrong.
- Item 4: Design doc; no code change until the redundancy is confirmed.
